content.text:
{Love one another with brotherly affection. Outdo one another in showing honor.}{Romans 12:10}
 {The inner product provides a geometry to abstract spaces.}{— Walter Rudin}

The **inner product** measures angles and lengths in a vector 
space.

content.title: Dot Product
content.content:
For vectors $\vect{u}, \vect{v} \in \R^n$, the **dot product** 
(or **inner product**) is:
\[
\vect{u} \cdot \vect{v} = \dotp{\vect{u}}{\vect{v}} = u_1v_1 + u_2v_2 + \cdots + u_nv_n = \vect{u}\transpose\vect{v}
\]

content.title: Properties of the Dot Product
content.content:
For vectors $\vect{u}, \vect{v}, \vect{w}$ and scalar $c$:

1. $\vect{u} \cdot \vect{v} = \vect{v} \cdot \vect{u}$ (symmetry)

2. $(\vect{u} + \vect{v}) \cdot \vect{w} = \vect{u} \cdot \vect{w} + \vect{v} \cdot \vect{w}$ (linearity)

3. $(c\vect{u}) \cdot \vect{v} = c(\vect{u} \cdot \vect{v})$

4. $\vect{u} \cdot \vect{u} \geq 0$, with equality iff $\vect{u} = \vect{0}$ (positive definiteness)
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Norm (Length)
content.content:
The **norm** or **length** of $\vect{v}$ is:
\[
\norm{\vect{v}} = \sqrt{\vect{v} \cdot \vect{v}} = \sqrt{v_1^2 + \cdots + v_n^2}
\]
A vector with $\norm{\vect{v}} = 1$ is called a **unit vector**.

marginNotes.text:
Behold, how good and pleasant it is 
when brothers dwell in unity!
